h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I’d always thought that was just me being sloppy
scored.statedInsightEvidence: that was the wrong frame because it makes it sound as if there is no rule at all
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was proceeding to simply fill out the conjugation grid to correct their supposedly sloppy speech for the essay.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought `p < .05` meant the change was important enough to act on, but that is doing too much work.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The dialogue was heading toward a straightforward arithmetic calculation of the new 0.4-second metric against business rollout costs.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I think my first instinct was still picturing one big flood doing the main work, because the canyon is so large.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I suppose that made me treat the flood mark as if it explained the whole map, which is probably too simple.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply answer the tutor's question about identifying the scar versus the removal route on the rock face.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still keep slipping and reading “treated as not guilty” as “believed not guilty”
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Now I think the correction is saying that is not really a belief about what happened
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to simply accept the tutor's clarification on burden and standard and finalize their note without further meta-cognitive reflection.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought fewer predators simply helped the deer because more of them survive, but with the paper deer crowded inside the same chalk boundary, that only explains the first round.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will simply append the new ecological concepts to their existing answer to complete the trophic cascade chain.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The clear water still makes me think the salt is gone, or at least not in there like before
scored.statedInsightEvidence: that was the problem with my earlier way of saying it because I was letting my eyes decide the whole thing
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply predict the outcome of the drying spoonful test based on the tutor's explanation of conservation.
